title: Simple Tomato Sauce
servings: Makes about 2 cups, enough for 4 servings of pasta

content: This is the sauce I make when I don't want to think. A can of good whole tomatoes, half an onion, butter, salt. That's basically it. Adapted loosely from Marcella Hazan.

sections.heading: Ingredients:
content: 1/2 medium yellow onion, peeled and halved
1 teaspoon kosher salt, plus more to taste
pinch of sugar (optional, if your tomatoes are acidic)

sections.heading: Directions:
content: Combine the tomatoes, onion, and butter in a medium saucepan. Crush the tomatoes lightly with your hands before adding, or use scissors to cut them up in the can. Add the salt.

Bring to a gentle simmer over medium heat, then reduce to low. Cook uncovered for about 45 minutes, stirring occasionally and breaking up the tomatoes further as they soften. The sauce should thicken and the fat should separate and pool on the surface a bit — that's fine, stir it back in.
Taste and adjust salt. Add a pinch of sugar if it tastes sharp.
Remove the onion halves (discard or eat them, they're delicious) and serve over pasta.
The sauce keeps refrigerated for 5 days or frozen for 3 months.
